PROMPT V9 — Full-Fidelity Legacy Extraction Prompt (Enhanced Contracts & Domain-Coupled Behaviors)

You are performing FULL EXTRACTION for a legacy TypeScript/JavaScript component that will be migrated to Rust. Your task is to generate a COMPLETE IR that preserves all domain AND domain-coupled operational semantics (including output message contracts, timing, duplication, error taxonomy, instrumentation, and configuration influences).

=================================
GOALS (What MUST be captured)
=================================
1. Public API surface (externally callable functions, classes, handlers, exported symbols) with signatures.
2. Output message/event contracts (every published/returned structure) with exact field set, types, optionality, transforms, serialization nuances, and duplication/ordering semantics.
3. Full error taxonomy (variants, codes, description patterns, mapping from underlying errors, propagation paths).
4. Domain logic AND domain-coupled operational behaviors that influence business outcomes (e.g. duplicate publish for schedule adherence, header setting affecting partitioning, sleep-based timing).
5. Observability contract (metrics, tracing identifiers, log markers) with trigger conditions and semantic intent.
6. Configuration & environment variable influence (variable → behavior impact).
7. Timing behaviors (sleep, interval, retry cadence, duplication loops) that affect interpretation, ordering, or downstream processing.
8. Test-derived invariants (assertions describing required outcomes—event counts, field transforms, filtering decisions, timing, ordering).
9. Precise type integrity: do NOT weaken types or make fields optional unless code already does.
10. Ambiguities: always include item, classify, and note—never omit due to uncertainty.

=================================
COMMENT HANDLING
=================================
Read ALL code AND comments.
- Contradiction: code wins.
- Vague/outdated/unhelpful: ignore.
- Clarifying + matches code: include.
- Describes unimplemented behavior: add to notes as "comment-claimed-but-not-implemented".
- Suggests incomplete enum: record existing variants; note possible incompleteness—DO NOT generalize type.

=================================
EXTRACTION STEPS
=================================
1. STATIC CONTEXT (Full lexical surface)
   - imports, exports
   - types, interfaces, enums
   - classes (methods, properties, static members)
   - functions (names, signatures)
   - constants
   - external libraries / service dependencies
   - deprecated or legacy references (from comments)
   - message headers used in publishing

2. SEMANTIC CONTEXT (Meaning & intent)
   - component purpose
   - data flow through modules
   - invariants & domain rules
   - interactions among types/modules
   - transforms applied to data (e.g. trimming, normalization)

3. EXECUTION CONTEXT (Runtime mechanics)
   - I/O operations (HTTP, Kafka, filesystem, key vault, identity, redis, etc.)
   - async patterns (promises, awaits, intervals, retries)
   - timers, scheduling, concurrency, locking
   - error propagation paths & wrapping logic
   - resource usage and lifecycle hooks

4. BUSINESS LOGIC (Domain decisions ONLY)
   Include rules that map input domain entities to output domain entities, validation predicates, filtering logic, movement classification, enrichment steps, field derivations.
   EXCLUDE pure infra (transport setup, generic HTTP adapter code) UNLESS its behavior alters domain interpretation (duplicate publish timing, header key strategy). Those become domain-coupled and MUST be captured under business_logic AND timing/observability contracts.

=================================
RETENTION RULES (Do NOT violate)
=================================
1. Preserve every output/event/message field exactly (name, type, optionality).
2. Preserve strong types/enums—do NOT widen to string/Option unless code does.
3. Preserve publish ordering, duplication loops, and sleep/timing semantics verbatim.
4. Preserve error codes and variant names—no renaming or grouping.
5. Preserve metric/tracing/log identifiers and increment/emission conditions.
6. Preserve environment variable influence; list defaults and behavioral effect.
7. Preserve transport headers that affect partitioning/routing (e.g. message key).
8. If uncertain classification (domain vs infra), include and mark classification=domain-coupled.
9. Do NOT invent logic; only minimal inference when directly implied by code & usage.
10. If unclear/missing, log in notes; DO NOT omit the reference.

=================================
AMBIGUITY HANDLING
=================================
If unsure whether a behavior influences business outcomes (e.g., duplicate publish, delay sleep, key header insertion): INCLUDE it under both timing_behaviors and domain_vs_infra_classification with classification=domain-coupled.

=================================
TEST-DERIVED INVARIANTS
=================================
Extract assertions that enforce outcomes (event count, field equality, filtering decisions, error variants). Translate each into a concise invariant. Treat as part of required domain behavior.

=================================
INSTRUMENTATION
=================================
Capture metrics/tracing calls (names, counters, gauges, attributes) and log markers. Record semantic purpose (e.g., "irrelevant station filter applied") and domain relevance (monitoring schedule adherence, data quality).

=================================
CONFIGURATION & ENV
=================================
List each env/config variable: name, default, source, usage effect (filtering, timing, topic naming, authentication). Link variables to flows/rules they affect.

=================================
IR SCHEMA (V9)
=================================
{
  "component_name": "",
  "source_paths": [],
  "step": "extract_all",
  "inputs": "Full source code of the component",
  "analysis": "",
  "ir_output": {
    "structures": {
      "imports": [],
      "exports": [],
      "types": [],
      "classes": [],
      "functions": [],
      "external_dependencies": [],
      "constants": []
    },
    "business_logic": {
      "flows": [],
      "rules": [],
      "state_transitions": [],
      "external_dependency_usage": []
    },
    "message_contracts": [
      {
        "name": "",
        "produced_by": "",
        "fields": [
          { "field": "", "type": "", "optional": false, "semantics": "", "source_enum": "", "transforms": [] }
        ],
        "transport_headers": [ { "name": "", "value_strategy": "static|derived|normalized" } ],
        "serialization_notes": "",
        "duplication_or_ordering_behavior": ""
      }
    ],
    "public_api": [
      { "symbol": "", "kind": "function|class|handler|trait", "signature": "", "description": "", "stability": "stable|internal|deprecated" }
    ],
    "error_contract": [
      { "variant": "", "code": "", "description_pattern": "", "origin_conditions": [], "maps_from": [] }
    ],
    "observability_contract": [
      { "instrument": "metric|tracing|log", "name": "", "trigger": "", "attributes": [], "domain_relevance": "" }
    ],
    "config_variables": [
      { "name": "", "default": "", "source": "env|file|code", "description": "", "affects": ["publishing|timing|filtering|validation|routing"] }
    ],
    "timing_behaviors": [
      { "pattern": "sleep|interval|duplicate_publish|retry_loop", "location": "", "duration_seconds": 0, "purpose": "", "domain_effect": "" }
    ],
    "test_invariants": [
      { "source_test": "", "assertion": "", "invariant": "", "domain_classification": "domain|domain-coupled" }
    ],
    "domain_vs_infra_classification": [
      { "item": "", "reason_included": "", "classification": "domain|infra|domain-coupled", "notes": "" }
    ],
    "context": {
      "purpose": "",
      "data_flow": [],
      "domain_rules": [],
      "interactions": [],
      "io_operations": [],
      "async_patterns": [],
      "error_flow": [],
      "performance_sensitive_areas": []
    },
    "notes": ""
  }
}

=================================
VALIDATION CHECKLIST BEFORE OUTPUT
=================================
- All published message/event structures listed in message_contracts.
- All error variants preserved with exact codes.
- All metrics/tracing/log identifiers present.
- All config/env vars with influence captured.
- All duplication/timing behaviors recorded.
- No field optionality added beyond code.
- Test assertions converted to invariants.
- Any ambiguity marked domain-coupled and not omitted.
- notes contains unresolved or speculative items only.

=================================
OUTPUT RULES
=================================
- Output MUST be valid JSON matching schema.
- Empty arrays if category absent; do NOT omit keys.
- No invented logic; minimal inference only when code use directly implies.
- Preserve ordering: structures first, then business_logic, then added sections.

=================================
FAILURE MODES TO AVOID
=================================
- Dropping fields from events.
- Renaming error codes.
- Omitting duplication timing semantics.
- Collapsing metrics to generic descriptions.
- Generalizing enums to strings.
- Ignoring header usage.

=================================
FINAL NOTE
=================================
If in doubt: INCLUDE and annotate—never silently discard.
